subject: [PATCH v2 079/110] ecryptfs: replace PRIino with %llu/%llx format strings

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author is addressing a concern about the PRIino format macro being replaced with concrete format strings, specifically that the replacement for %PRIino in fs/inode.c was incorrect and should be %llx instead of %llu.

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch removes the kino_t typedef and PRIino format macro, but did not explicitly request any changes or express disagreement.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks good. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch removes the kino_t typedef and PRIino format macro from the Linux kernel, replacing them with u64 type and format strings respectively. The changes are made throughout the tree, including in various file system-related files such as fs/dcache.c, fs/eventpoll.c, and include/linux/fs.h. The removal of these macros is necessary because i_ino has been widened to a 64-bit unsigned integer (u64), making the typedef and macro redundant.
